name: How do I prove my injury is permanent and get a settlement?

mainEntity.name: How do I prove my injury is permanent and get a settlement?

acceptedAnswer.text: You prove permanent disability through a medical 'impairment rating' once you reach Maximum Medical Improvement. This rating determines your eligibility for a settlement based on the percentage of permanent loss of function.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Proving a permanent disability requires a medical determination that you have reached Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI) and still possess a lasting physical or mental impairment. Once your healing has plateaued, a doctor will perform an impairment rating exam using the AMA Guides to the Evaluation of Permanent Impairment. This results in a percentage rating (e.g., a 10% whole-body impairment), which translates into a specific dollar amount based on state law.

The evidence you need:
- Comprehensive medical records from your primary treating physician.
- A formal Permanent Partial Disability (PPD) rating.
- Vocational assessments if you cannot return to your previous line of work.

Do not assume the insurance company’s doctor will give you a fair rating. They often downplay the severity of permanent restrictions. You have the right to a second opinion, and an attorney can help ensure the rating accurately reflects your life-long limitations. Proper documentation of how the injury affects daily life is crucial.
